# my-auth-library A small library for handling user authentication in Go. ## Usage To log in a user, call the Login function with a username and password. The function will return a session token on success. ```go err := auth.Login(username, password) if err != nil { return err } ``` To sign up a new user, use the Register function. It validates the email and stores a hashed password. ```go err := auth.Register(email, password) ``` ## API The library exposes three main functions: Login, Register, and Logout. Each is documented in the godoc page linked from the project README. ## Examples The examples directory contains a working demo that wires a sign-in form and a sign-up form to a SQLite store. Run it with `go run ./examples/web`. ## Security notes Passwords are stored with bcrypt. Sessions are stored as opaque tokens, not JWTs. Welcome contributions — please open an issue before submitting a PR. ## Contributing Fork the repository, create a feature branch, and submit a pull request. All contributors must agree to the project's code of conduct. ## License MIT.
